content.full_text:
California
Fair P01itical
Practices Cornrnission
May l-, 1990
Patricia D. Sinclair
corporate Secretary
South Montebello Irrigation District
864 west Washington Boulevard
Montebello, cA 90640
Re: Your Request for Inforrnal Assistance
our File No. I-9 0-07 0
Dear Ms. sinclair:
This letter j,s in response to your request for advice regard-
ing application of the Political Reform Act (the rrActr') r
to elected officials, 
employees, and consultants who manage public
investments for your agency. You have not provided us with the
nanes of the public officials 
whose duties are in question, nor is
it stated in your letter that those persons have authorized your
request for advice. Accordingly, we consider your letter to be a
request for informal assistance pursuant to Regulation L8329(c)
(copy enclosed . ) z
OUESTIONS
1. Does the Act require that the
file a Statenent of Econonic fnterests
2. Does the Act require that the
file a Statement of Economic Interests
treasurer of the district
with the commission?
secretary of the district
with the Commission?
3. Does the Act require that Trust Services of America,
which directs investment of the district's 
pension funds, f il-e a
statement of Econonic Interests with the Commission?
1 Go.r"r.r."nt code Sections 81ooo-91015. A11 statutory references
are to the Government Code unl,ess otherwise indicated. 
Cornmission
regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations Section
18OOO, et seq. Al-I references to regulations are to Title 2,
Division 6 of the California code of Regulations.
2 Informaf assistance does not provide the requestor r^rith the
inmunity provided by an opinion or formal written advj"ce. (sec-
tion 8311-4; Regulation 18329 (c) . )
428 J Street, Suite 8OO . P.O. Box 8O7 o Sacrarnento CA 958O4-O8O7 . (916) 322-5660


File No. r-9 O- 07 O
Page 2
4. Your pension fund is insured under ERISA. A consultant
provides actuarial services to your pension committee and aids in
the preparation of tax returns and other forms required by the
federal government. Does the Act require this consultant to file
a Statement of Economic fnterests with the Cornnission?
5. For purposes of the Act, does rrincomerr include salary
from employment?
CONCLUS IONS
l-4. 
As recently amended, the Act requires that certain
elected officials, 
appointed public officials 
and t'other publ j.c
officials 
e/ho manage public investments'r file Statements of
Economj,c fnterests. 
we are currently in the process of determin-
ing whether the individuals serving in the positions you describe
will be considered trother public officials 
who manage pubtic
investments.rt A regulation to cl-arify this term was scheduled for
possible pre-notice discussion at the April 3 neeting of the Com-
mission. At that meeting the Commission directed staff to
continue to lrork with interested persons on this issue. 
We
anticipate that a rreeting of interested persons wj.ll- be hel-d in
the near future and that the staff will present a revised proposal
to the Commission this summer.
5. For purposes of the disclosure requirenents of the Act,
rrincometr does not include salary and reimbursement for expenses or
per dien received by an employee of a state, local, or federal
government agency from the agency. Salary frorn other employers is
incone and must be disclosed.
FACTS
You are the corporate secretary for the South Montebello Ir-
rj-gation Oistrict. 
fn that capacity, you seek our advice to
determine the filing 
and disclosure obJ.igations of ernployees and
consultants with your agency lrho manage public investrnents.
The treasurer of the district 
cannot make public investrnents
without the authorization of the board of directors of the
district. 
Any authorization given is entered into the minutes of
the district 
as a board directive authorizing and directing the
treasurer to make investments as set out by the board.
The pension fund of the district 
is insured under ERISA. The
fund is managed by a pension cornmittee composed of two board
members and the secretary of the district. 
The funds are
deposited with Trust Services of America which directs the j.nvest-
ments. The district 
also employs a consultant who provides
actuarial services to your pension committee and aids in the
preparation of tax returns and other forms required by the federal
government. This consultant advises the pension committee frorn
time to tirne.


File No. I-90-07o
Page 3
ANALYSIS
The Political- Reform Act was enacted by the people of the
State of california by initiative 
in 1974. The purpose for the
disclosure and disqua 1i f ication provisions of the Act is to ensure
that public officials, 
whether elected or appointed, perform their
duties in an irnpartial manner, free from bias caused by their own
financial- interests or the financial interests of persons \"/ho have
supported thern. (Section 81001(b) . )
Thus, under the Act, public officials 
must disclose their
assets and income which rnay be rnaterially affected by their deci-
sions. (Section 8l-002(c).) Section 87200 provides that certain
high-Ieve1 elected and appointed public officials, 
and candidates
for those positions, nust file statements of Economic Interests
providing broad disclosure of their economic interests. 
Effective
January L, L99o, Section 87200 has been anended to extend its
financial disclosure requirernents to city and county treasurers
and rrother public off icial,s who manage public investments.rl
Elected officials 
and employees of public agencies who direct or
control or otherwi-se make decisions l^rith respect to the investrnent
of public moneys are rrpublic officials 
who manage public invest-
ments.rr Accordingly, any person who fal1s within one of these
categories, or is a candidate for any of these offices, is now
subject to the broad conf l ict-of-interest 
disclosure requirements
of Articfe 2 of chapter 7 of the Act.
Previous to the amendment of Section 872oo, city and county
treasurers and other officials 
who handled investnents for a
public entity should have been designated in their agencies'
conflict of interest codes and should have filed Forn 730 with
their agencies. These officials 
must now file Forn 721 v,rhich
requirei greater disclosure of the officials' 
economic interests.3
You have asked whether individuals serving in certain
capacities in your district 
are subject to the provj.sions of Sec-
tion 87200. In the past we have concluded that consultants are
subject to the disclosure provisions of the Act. 
(See Havden
Advice Letter, No. A-84-319, copy enclosed.) Hor,rever, at this
tirne, these issues are before the Commissj.on for consideration.
3 Cornmission staff have advised that those persons wfio }tave been
filing on Form 730 nay use that form to file in 1990. They will
begin filing on Form 721- commencing with the 1990 statement, filed
in 1991. New filers wiII file Form 721 cornmencing with the 1989
statements. The filing 
deadLine for new filers is 30 days after
assuming office or by January 31, 1990, if they have assumed of-
fice on or before January 1, 1990.


File No. I-9o-07o
Page 4
At its Aprl] meeting the Commission considered the scope of ap-
plication of section 87200 vrith regTard to "other public officials
who manage public investments. rr we have included your name on a
list of interested persons who t^riIl be kept informed of the
progress of regulations concerning this issue.'
You have also asked v/hat constitutes 'rincomerr for purposes of
the disclosure provisions of the Act. Under the provisions of the
Act, "i-ncomerr does not include salary and reimbursement for
expenses or per diem received by an employee of a state, Iocal, or
federal government agency from the agency. (section 82030(b) (2).)
Therefore, "inconerr does not include any salary or per diem a
filer receives from the district. 
Salary from other employers is
income and must be disclosed.
I trust this letter adequately responds to your inquiry,
Shoufd you have any further questions regarding this matter, do
not hesitate to contact ne at (915) 322-5901.
S incerely,
Kathryn E. Donovan
General counsel

By: Blanca M. Breeze
counsef, Legal Division
KED:BMB:p■h
Enclosures
4 Ir, ..,y event, if any of these individuals makes or participates
in the making of decisions which may foreseeably have a material
effect on any financial interest, they should be included as
designated enployees in your district's 
conflict-of-interest 
code.
(Sections 82019 and 87300, et seq.; Regulation 18700, copy
enclosed.) As consultants, they would be subject to the
disclosure and disqual if ication provisions of the Act. 
(Sections
87LOO-873L3; Hsu Advice Letter, No. I-89-485, copy enclosed.)
